title: Discovery Runtime Behaviour Proof

crawlModeEvidence.phase1_sitemap: discoverSitemaps() + crawlSitemaps() found 9 URLs from sitemap.xml (sitemap-parser.ts:73-105, 144-166)
crawlModeEvidence.phase2_sitemap_seed: 9 sitemap entries seeded into BFS queue at depth=0 (discovery-engine.ts:70-81)
crawlModeEvidence.phase3_homepage: crawlPage(baseUrl, 0, null, 'header-nav') crawled homepage at depth=0 (discovery-engine.ts:84)
crawlModeEvidence.phase4_bfs: bfsCrawl() ran but queue was empty — all links from homepage already visited (discovery-engine.ts:87)

recursiveCrawlEvidence: All 9 sitemap URLs were already in this.visited before homepage crawl discovered them. The homepage crawlPage() at depth 0 found internal links (header-nav, footer-nav, page-body), but normalizeUrl() + this.visited.has() check (discovery-engine.ts:115) returned true for all of them. Queue remained empty. BFS loop (discovery-engine.ts:192) terminated immediately.
browserLayerEvidence: Page extraction uses fetchText() (Node.js fetch) in page-extractor.ts:261. No Chrome DevTools MCP, no Puppeteer, no Playwright is called during discovery. The only browser reference is in detail extraction (Layer 3), not Layer 1 discovery.

urlCountExplanation.reason: Site sitemap contained exactly 9 URLs. Homepage added 1. All internal links from homepage matched already-visited URLs.
urlCountExplanation.sitemapOnly: Hybrid — sitemap provided base set, homepage crawl found no additional URLs
